title: GlassBox lesson (authoring contract)
description: Documents the structure of a lessons/<topic>/<register>.lesson.toml file. The human layer, kept separate from the trace. This schema is the human-readable contract; tracer/build.py enforces the same rules in Python (with named, contextual errors) and merges notes into the trace by step index. It is NOT run by Ajv (lessons are TOML, not JSON).

language.description: Only 'python' is supported in v1; defaults to 'python'.

domain_model.description: Defaults to 'execution-derived' (the program IS the subject). Set 'author-asserted-simulation' when the program is a toy model of a larger real system (e.g. a routing/DNS/scheduler sim): the trace stays real, but the claim that it models the real world is yours, and the build gate requires this for network/gantt/dns abstractions.

source.description: The Python program to trace (TOML multi-line string).

inputs.description: Optional names predefined in the program's global scope (e.g. a list to search). They appear in state like any other variable.

bind.description: Maps a variable name to an abstraction slot. Every bound variable must appear in the trace at some step, or the build fails.

notes.description: Author-asserted per-step meaning, merged into the trace by step index.

checkpoints.description: Steps at which the player gates forward motion and asks the learner to predict the NEXT step's state. The real next state adjudicates — authors pose the question (`ask`), never the answer. `step` must have a successor (it cannot be the last step).
